zh.Warwickshire, England: 英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Beauchamp於大約1342年出生時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick30歲，母親Katherine Mortimer30歲。她與Sir Roger de Umfraville結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Warwickshire, England: 英国英格兰瓦維其可爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Beauchamp于大约1342年出生时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick30岁，母亲Katherine Mortimer30岁。她与Sir Roger de Umfraville结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Beauchamp was born about 1342, her father, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, was 30 and her mother, Katherine Mortimer, was 30. She married Sir Roger de Umfraville. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Beauchamp est née environ 1342, son père, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Katherine Mortimer, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Roger de Umfraville. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Beauchamp um 1342 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick 30 und ihre Mutter Katherine Mortimer 30. Sie hat Sir Roger de Umfraville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Beauchamp è nata il circa 1342, sua padre, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Katherine Mortimer, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Sir Roger de Umfraville. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Beauchampが約1342年に生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwickは30歳，母Katherine Mortimerは30歳であった。Sir Roger de Umfravilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1342년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Mortimer(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 Sir Roger de Umfraville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1342, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Mortimer, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Roger de Umfraville. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Beauchamp родилась около 1342 г., её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Mortimer, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Roger de Umfraville. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1342, su papá, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Katherine Mortimer, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Sir Roger de Umfraville. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
